differences.details: Production is a compact mobile single-column flow; prototype is a wide desktop modal with larger gutters, wider cards, and multi-column sections, so spacing rhythm does not match.
Production typography is heavier and more compact (especially title/numbers); prototype uses lighter text weights and larger line heights, reducing visual density.
Accent system differs: production leans yellow/cyan/purple accents, while prototype uses red CTAs and green win text with different emphasis mapping.
Production cards/pills are generally more rounded; prototype corners are comparatively sharper and less consistent across components.
Prototype uses stronger glow/vignette effects; production shadows are subtler and flatter, so depth treatment is mismatched.
Prototype uses large cutout player portraits and larger hero tiles; production uses smaller circular avatars and tighter image blocks.
Primary emphasis differs: production prioritizes quick stats + next match in a compact scan pattern; prototype prioritizes hero header + large roster strip + bottom CTA.

top_3_fixes: Match production layout first: switch prototype to mobile-width single-column structure with production-like section order and tighter vertical spacing tokens.
Normalize visual tokens: align accent palette, font weights/sizes, corner radii, and shadow intensity to production component-by-component.
Resize media and hierarchy: reduce portrait/avatar scale, use compact circular player images, and rebalance emphasis toward stats/next-match blocks instead of oversized hero/CTA areas.
